text.title: JPA / Hibernate Failure Modes
text.summary: 6 antipatterns observed in real Spring Data JPA + Hibernate deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Adding an entity to a `Set` triggers infinite recursion and a `StackOverflowError`; or `Set` semantics break (duplicates allowed).
antipattern.root-cause: `@Data` generates `equals`/`hashCode` over all fields, including lazy collections; touching a collection loads it from the DB and recurses.
antipattern.fix: Replace `@Data` with `@Getter @Setter`. Implement `equals`/`hashCode` manually over a business key per `business-key-equality` rule.

antipattern.symptom: Query returns 5MB JSON or throws `MultipleBagFetchException`; profile shows multi-million-row Cartesian join.
antipattern.root-cause: Author set `FetchType.EAGER` on an association "to be safe"; combined with another EAGER collection it explodes.
antipattern.fix: Switch all associations to LAZY; do per-query `JOIN FETCH` or `@EntityGraph` when callers actually need the data.

antipattern.symptom: Deleting an `OrderLine` cascades to the parent `Order` and wipes the entire order plus all sibling lines.
antipattern.root-cause: `CascadeType.ALL` on a `@ManyToOne` propagates persist/merge/remove up the ownership chain â€” semantically backwards.
antipattern.fix: Remove cascade from `@ManyToOne`. Cascade is only appropriate from parent to children on `@OneToMany`.

antipattern.symptom: Deploy aborts with `SchemaManagementException: missing column ...` or â€” worse â€” `ddl-auto=update` silently alters the prod schema.
antipattern.root-cause: Entity diff merged without a paired Flyway migration.
antipattern.fix: Enforce `ddl-auto=validate` in prod profile; gate PRs on a `validate-paired-migration.py` script that fails if an entity diff lands without a `db/migration/V*.sql` change.

antipattern.symptom: Bulk update reports `n` rows updated; subsequent `findById` returns the pre-update entity; logic that depends on the new state breaks silently.
antipattern.root-cause: `@Modifying` JPQL bypasses Hibernate's first-level cache; without `clearAutomatically`, stale entities linger in the persistence context.
antipattern.fix: Annotate `@Modifying(clearAutomatically = true, flushAutomatically = true)` on every bulk update/delete repository method.

antipattern.symptom: Repository test suite takes 90 s to boot; CI flakes when the full context cache exceeds the JVM heap.
antipattern.root-cause: Author used `@SpringBootTest` for what should be a repository-only test, loading the entire web + security context.
antipattern.fix: Switch to `@DataJpaTest` + Testcontainers; reserve `@SpringBootTest` for cross-layer integration tests where the full stack is the SUT.
